texto: Queer, how her own desperate need of light seemed to throw such brilliance over the affairs of the members of her family. She carried her need like a many-batteried pocket spotlight, illuminating emotional corners in other people, but she walked in darkness behind it. Her wrist wouldn't bend to turn it on herself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Helen R. Hull: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Queer, how her own desperate need of light seemed to throw such brilliance over the affairs of th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, light, persephone pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao-noturna.
